summarization.a: The figure displays X-ray diffraction patterns of samples annealed at different temperatures (200°C, 400°C, 600°C, and 700°C) with peaks corresponding to TiN, Cu, and Cu₃Si phases.

a.question: What was the thickness of the copper layer in the stack?
What was the thickness of the TiN layer in the stack?
At which temperatures were the Cu-TiN-Si stacks annealed at?
How does annealing affect the Cu-TiN-Si stack?
a.answer: When compared to the reference powder samples of cubic TiN, Cu, and Cu3Si, the TiN lines are not affected by the annealing temperature. The Cu lines become narrower and more intense as temperature increases. Lastly, even at 700 °C, no peaks corresponding to Cu3Si are observed, indicating that barrier failure does not occur.
